commands.review_summary:
This PR changes the Codex app-server event projector to call `onReasoningStream` with accumulated reasoning text and adds a regression test for progressive accumulation.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +23. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main sends raw Codex reasoning deltas, while Discord treats formatted reasoning payloads as full snapshots and replaces prior progress. I did not run a live Discord or Telegram session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof from the actual channel/runtime path, not a mechanical code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): send accumulated reasoning text in onReasoningStream
target.body:
## Summary

The Codex app-server event-projector sent raw reasoning deltas to `onReasoningStream` instead of the accumulated text. Both the native pi-embedded runtime (`pi-embedded-subscribe.ts:952`) and the btw runtime (`btw.ts:543`) send accumulated snapshots, so downstream consumers (Discord, Telegram) treat each callback as a complete reasoning snapshot.

The mismatch caused Discord's `mergeReasoningProgressText` to detect each tiny delta as a standalone `"Thinking\n\n_<delta>_"` snapshot via `isReasoningSnapshotText`, replacing the previous content instead of appending. The visible result degraded to a single bullet with only the final delta chunk (e.g., `• !`).

## Changes

**`extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts`** (1 line):
- `handleReasoningDelta` now sends the accumulated text from `reasoningTextByItem` instead of the raw `delta`, matching the contract of both the native and btw runtimes.

**`extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`** (1 new test):
- Added "sends accumulated reasoning text instead of raw deltas" test that sends 3 sequential reasoning deltas and verifies each `onReasoningStream` call receives the progressively accumulated text.

## Verification

- **Behavior addressed**: Discord reasoning progress overwrites prior chunks, degrading to a single character display, because Codex event-projector sends raw deltas instead of accumulated snapshots
- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, Node 22, vitest
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ |extensions| ../../extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts (70 tests) 295ms

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  70 passed (70)
  ```
  New test `sends accumulated reasoning text instead of raw deltas` verifies:
  - 3 sequential deltas ("Reading ", "the ", "files") produce 3 callbacks
  - Call 1 receives `{ text: "Reading " }`
  - Call 2 receives `{ text: "Reading the " }` (accumulated, not raw delta)
  - Call 3 receives `{ text: "Reading the files" }` (full accumulated text)

  Before fix, each call received only the raw delta (`"Reading "`, `"the "`, `"files"`), causing Discord's snapshot-replacement to show only the final chunk.
- **Observed result after fix**: each `onReasoningStream` callback receives the progressively accumulated reasoning text, matching the contract established by the native pi-embedded and btw runtimes
- **What was not tested**: live Discord channel rendering with a running Codex app-server session (requires active Codex backend and Discord bot)

Closes #83983
target.author: sebtardif
